tables.description: This subject phenotype table includes subject IDs, sex, age, marital status, and baseline information, including ever had a hemoccult test, sigmoidoscopy, colonoscopy, barium enema/x-ray test, CT colonography or virtual colonoscopy test, polyps in large bowel, colon or rectum, familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP), Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, irritable bowel syndrome, diverticular disease, large bowel or colon removed, large bowel or colon completely removed or only part of it removed, gallbladder removed, diabetes, high cholesterol, high levels of triglycerides, any type of cancer, etc.

variables.name: ACETAMIN
variables.description: Have you ever taken acetaminophen (such as Tylenol, Anacin-3, and Panadol) at least twice a week for more than a month?

variables.name: ACET_FRQ
variables.description: When you were taking acetaminophen regularly, how often did you take it?

variables.name: ACET_INT
variables.description: Interval in which acetaminophen was taken.

variables.name: ACET_LEN
variables.description: How long, in total, have you taken acetaminophen?

variables.name: ACET_REG
variables.description: About two years ago were you taking acetaminophen regularly?

variables.name: ACET_TIME
variables.description: Interval for total time acetaminophen was taken.

variables.name: AE_AGE
variables.description: (for females only) How old were you when you first took tamoxifen, raloxifene or other anti-estrogen medication?

variables.name: AE_CONT
variables.description: (for females only) Were you taking tamoxifen, raloxifene, or other anti-estrogen medications about 2 years ago?

variables.name: AE_LEN
variables.description: (for females only) In total, how long did you take tamoxifen, raloxifene, or other anti-estrogen medications?

variables.name: AE_OTHER
variables.description: (for females only) Did you take other anti-estrogen medication?

variables.name: AE_RALOXIFENE
variables.description: (for females only) Did you take raloxifene?

variables.name: AE_TAMOXIFEN
variables.description: (for females only) Did you take tamoxifen?

variables.name: AE_TIME
variables.description: (for females only) Interval of frequency tamoxifen, raloxifene, or other anti-estrogen medications were taken.

variables.name: AGEDX1
variables.description: How old were you when a doctor first told you that you had cancer.

variables.name: AGEDX2
variables.description: How old were you when a doctor first told you that you had cancer.

variables.name: AGEDX3
variables.description: How old were you when a doctor first told you that you had cancer.

variables.name: AGEDX4
variables.description: How old were you when a doctor first told you that you had cancer.

variables.name: AGE_EPI
variables.description: What is your age (years)?

variables.name: ALCOH20
variables.description: When you were in your 20s, did you typically consume any alcoholic beverages at least once a week for 6 months or longer? (Alcoholic beverages include beer, hard cider, wine, sake, liquor, spirits, mixed drinks, or cocktails.)

variables.name: ALCOH20_PW
variables.description: How many alcoholic beverages a week did you typically consume during your 20s? (Probe: How many 4-oz. glasses of wine, or 12-oz. cans or bottles of beer or hard cider, or 1-oz. servings of sake or liquor {spirits} did you consume?)

variables.name: ALCOH20_YRS
variables.description: How many years in total did you consume at least one alcoholic beverage a week in your 20s?

variables.name: ALCOH30
variables.description: Did you typically consume any alcoholic beverages at least once a week for 6 months or longer in your 30s and 40s? (Alcoholic beverages include beer, hard cider, wine, sake, liquor, spirits, mixed drinks, or cocktails.)

variables.name: ALCOH30_PW
variables.description: How many alcoholic beverages (4-oz. glasses of wine, 12-oz.cans/bottles of beer or hard cider, or 1-oz. servings of sake or liquor {spirits}) a week did you consume during those years in your 30s and 40s?

variables.name: ALCOH30_YRS
variables.description: How many years in total did you consume at least one alcoholic beverage a week in your 30s and 40s?

variables.name: ALCOH50
variables.description: Did you typically consume any alcoholic beverages, including beer, hard cider, wine, sake, liquor, spirits, mixed drinks, or cocktails, at least once a week for 6 months or longer since turning 50?

variables.name: ALCOH50_PW
variables.description: How many alcoholic beverages ( 4-oz. glasses of wine, 12-oz. cans/bottles of beer or hard cider, or 1-oz. servings of sake or liquor) a week did you typically consume during those years since turning 50?

variables.name: ALCOH50_YRS
variables.description: How many years in total did you consume at least one alcoholic beverage a week since turning 50?

variables.name: ANTACIDS
variables.description: Have you ever taken calcium-based antacids (such as Tums, Rolaids, Extra-Strength Rolaids, Alka-Mints, and Chooz Antacid gum) at least twice a week for more than a month?

variables.name: ANTACID_FRQ
variables.description: When you were taking calcium-based antacids regularly, how often did you take them?

variables.name: ANTACID_INT
variables.description: Interval for frequency calcium-based antacids were taken.

variables.name: ANTACID_LEN
variables.description: How long, in total, have you taken calcium-based antacids?

variables.name: ANTACID_REG
variables.description: About two years ago were you taking calcium-based antacids regularly?

variables.name: ANTACID_TIME
variables.description: Interval for total time calcium-based antacids were taken.

variables.name: ANTI_ESTROGEN
variables.description: (for females only) Have you ever taken tamoxifen, raloxifene, or other anti-estrogen medication (such as Lupron or Depo-Provera)?

variables.name: ASPIRIN
variables.description: Have you ever taken aspirin (such as Anacin, Bufferin, Bayer, Excedrin, and Ecotrin) at least twice a week for more than a month?

variables.name: ASPIRIN_FRQ
variables.description: When you were taking aspirin regularly, how often did you take it?

variables.name: ASPIRIN_INT
variables.description: Interval in which aspirin was taken.

variables.name: ASPIRIN_LEN
variables.description: How long, in total, have you taken aspirin?

variables.name: ASPIRIN_REG
variables.description: About two years ago were you taking aspirin regularly?

variables.name: ASPIRIN_TIME
variables.description: Interval for total time aspirin was taken.

variables.name: AUS_EDUCATION
variables.description: What is the highest level of education that you have completed (Australia only)?

variables.name: BARIUM
variables.description: Have you ever had a barium enema/x-ray test? A barium enema (BE) is an x-ray examination of your colon. In this procedure a special solution, and generally air is pumped into the colon or bowel through the rectum, so these organs can be seen on the x-ray.

variables.name: BARIUM_NO

variables.description: Have you ever taken calcium pills or tablets (not including antacids) at least twice a week for more than a month?

variables.name: CALCIUM_FRQ

variables.name: CALCIUM_INT

variables.description: Interval of frequency of a serving of chicken.

variables.name: CHICKEN_OUT
variables.description: On average, when you ate chicken cooked by pan-frying, broiling, grilling, or barbecuing, which of the following best describes its outside appearance?

variables.name: CHOLES_MED

variables.name: CIDER30_AMT
variables.description: How many 12-oz cans/bottles of hard cider did you consume at least once a week for 6 months or longer in your 30s and 40s?

variables.name: CIDER30_INT
variables.description: Interval of frequency of hard cider consumed at least once a week for 6 months or longer in your 30s and 40s?

variables.name: CIDER50_YRS
variables.description: How many years did you consume hard cider at least once a week for 6 months or longer since turning 50?

variables.name: CIG
variables.description: Have you ever smoked at least one cigarette a day for 3 months or longer?

variables.name: CIGAR
variables.description: Have you ever smoked at least 1 cigar per month for at least 3 months?

variables.name: CIGAR_1ST_AGE
variables.description: How old were you when you first started smoking at least one cigar per month?

variables.name: CIGAR_2YRS
variables.description: Two years ago, were you still smoking at least one cigar a month?

variables.name: CIGAR_CURR
variables.description: Do you still smoke at least one cigar a month?

variables.name: CIGAR_NO
variables.description: During periods when you smoked regularly, how many cigars did you typically smoke in a month? ("Regularly" means at least one cigar a month.)

variables.name: CIGAR_STOP_AGE
variables.description: When did you permanently stop smoking at least one cigar a month?

variables.name: CIGAR_YRS
variables.description: How many years in total did you smoke at least one cigar a month? (If you have stopped and restarted at least once, count only the time when you were smoking.)

variables.name: CIG_1ST_AGE
variables.description: How old were you when you first started smoking at least one cigarette a day for 3 months or longer?

variables.name: CIG_2YRS
variables.description: About 2 years ago were you still smoking at least one cigarette a day?

variables.name: CIG_CURR
variables.description: Do you still smoke at least one cigarette a day?

variables.name: CIG_PD
variables.description: During periods when you smoked regularly, how many cigarettes did you typically smoke in a day? (1 pack = 20 cigarettes.)

variables.name: CIG_STOP_AGE
variables.description: When did you permanently stop smoking at least 1 cigarette a day?

variables.name: CIG_YRS
variables.description: How many years in total did you smoke at least one cigarette per day for 3 months or longer? (If you stopped and restarted at least once, count only the time when you were smoking.)

variables.name: COLITIS
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you had ulcerative colitis? (This is an inflammation and ulceration of the lining of the bowel (colon) and rectum. It is not a stomach ulcer.)

variables.name: COLITIS_1ST_AGE
variables.description: Hold old were you when your doctor first told you that you had ulcerative colitis?

variables.name: COLON_REM
variables.description: Have you ever had any of your large bowel or colon removed?

variables.name: COLSCOPE
variables.description: Have you ever had a colonoscopy? (A colonoscopy is an examination of the entire large bowel using a long flexible instrument. This examination is usually done under sedation).

variables.name: COLSCOPE_1ST_AGE
variables.description: How old were you when you had your first colonoscopy?

variables.name: COLSCOPE_LST_AGE
variables.description: How old were you when you had your last colonoscopy?

variables.name: COLSCOPE_NO
variables.description: How many separate colonoscopies have you had?

variables.name: COL_REM_1ST_AGE
variables.description: How old were you when you first had any of your bowel or colon removed?

variables.name: COL_REM_EXT
variables.description: Was your large bowel or colon completely removed or was only part of it removed?

variables.name: COL_REM_LST_AGE
variables.description: How old were you when you last had the operation to remove all or part of your bowel or colon?

variables.name: COL_SURG
variables.description: Have you had more than one surgery to remove your bowel or colon?

variables.name: COX2
variables.description: Have you ever taken a Cox-II inhibitor (such as Celebrex, Celecoxib, Vioxx, Rofecoxib, Bextra, or Valdecoxib) at least twice a week for more than a month?

variables.name: COX2_FRQ
variables.description: When you were taking a Cox-II inhibitor regularly, how often did you take it?

variables.name: COX2_INT
variables.description: Interval in which the Cox-II inhibitor was taken.

variables.name: COX2_LEN
variables.description: How long, in total, have you taken a Cox-II inhibitor?

variables.name: COX2_REG
variables.description: About two years ago were you taking a Cox-II inhibitor regularly?

variables.name: COX2_TIME
variables.description: Interval for total time a Cox-II inhibitor was taken.

variables.name: CROHNS
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you had Crohn's disease? (This is where you have an inflammation that extends into the deeper layers of the intestinal wall. It may also affect other parts of the digestive tract, including the mouth, esophagus, stomach and small intestine.)

variables.name: CROHNS_1ST_AGE
variables.description: How old were you when your doctor first told you that you had Crohn's disease?

variables.name: DIABETES
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you had diabetes, also known as diabetes mellitus? (Note: this does not include gestational diabetes/diabetes you had only during pregnancy)

variables.name: DIAB_AGE
variables.description: How old were you when your doctor first told you that you had diabetes?

variables.name: DIAB_MEDS
variables.description: Did you ever take medication to control your diabetes?

variables.name: DIAB_MED_TYP
variables.description: What type of medication did you use: pills or insulin injections?

variables.name: DIVERTIC
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you had diverticular disease? (This may also be called diverticulosis or diverticulitis. It's a condition in which the bowel may become infected and can lead to pain and chronic problems with bowel habits.)

variables.name: DIVERTIC_AGE
variables.description: How old were you when a doctor first told you that you had diverticular disease?

variables.name: D_INJ_CONT
variables.description: Were you still taking insulin injections for diabetes about two years ago?

variables.name: D_INJ_FRQ
variables.description: How often did you take insulin injections (for diabetes)?

variables.name: D_INJ_INT
variables.description: Interval for frequency of injections taken for diabetes.

variables.name: D_INJ_LEN
variables.description: How long in total have you taken insulin injections for diabetes?

variables.name: D_INJ_TIME
variables.description: Interval for total time diabetes insulin injections was taken.

variables.name: D_PILLS_CONT
variables.description: Were you still taking pills for diabetes about two years ago?

variables.name: D_PILLS_FRQ
variables.description: How often did you take pills (for diabetes)?

variables.name: D_PILLS_INT
variables.description: Interval for frequency of pills taken for diabetes.

variables.name: D_PILLS_LEN
variables.description: How long in total have you taken pills for diabetes?

variables.name: D_PILLS_TIME
variables.description: Interval for total time that diabetes pills were taken.

variables.name: D_PUMP_CONT
variables.description: Were you still using insulin pump for diabetes about two years ago?

variables.name: D_PUMP_FRQ
variables.description: How often did you use medical pump (for diabetes)?

variables.name: D_PUMP_INT
variables.description: Interval for frequency of insulin pump used for diabetes.

variables.name: D_PUMP_LEN
variables.description: How long in total have you used insulin pump for diabetes?

variables.name: D_PUMP_TIME
variables.description: Interval for total time diabetes insulin pump was taken.

variables.name: EDUCATION
variables.description: What is the highest level of education that you have completed (for US and Canada only)?

variables.name: EO_AGE
variables.description: (for females only) How old were you when you first took estrogen only medication?

variables.name: EO_CONT
variables.description: (for females only) Were you still taking estrogen-only medication about 2 years ago?

variables.name: EO_LEN
variables.description: (for females only) In total, how long did you take estrogen-only medication?

variables.name: EO_TIME
variables.description: (for females only) Interval for frequency of estrogen-only medication taken.

variables.name: EPI_Q_AGE
variables.description: Calculated age epidemiology questionnaire completed/administered or questionnaire received by center. Use date completed/administered. Only use age questionnaire received if date completed not available.

variables.name: EPI_Q_CMPLTYPE
variables.description: Completion age type. Indicates whether the value in field EPI_Q_AGE represents calculated age interview conducted/completed or calculated age questionnaire returned/received.

variables.name: ESTROGEN_ONLY
variables.description: (for females only) Were you prescribed an estrogen-only pill or patch (such as Premarin)?

variables.name: ETHNIC_BL
variables.description: Do you consider yourself to be Hispanic/Latino?

variables.name: FAP
variables.description: Has a doctor ever told you that you had familial adenomatous polyposis (FAP)? (This is a condition, sometimes occurring in families, in which numerous polyps line the inside of the large bowel or colon)

variables.name: FAP_1ST_AGE
variables.description: How old were you when your doctor first told you that you had FAP?

variables.name: FA_FRQ
variables.description: When you were taking folic acid or folate pills or tablets regularly, how often did you take it?

variables.name: FA_INT
variables.description: Interval for frequency folic acid or folate pills were taken.

variables.name: FA_LEN
variables.description: How long, in total, have you taken folic acid or folate pills or tablets?

variables.name: FA_REG
variables.description: About two years ago were you taking folic acid or folate pills or tablets regularly?

variables.name: FA_TIME
variables.description: Interval for total amount of time folic acid or folate pills or tablets were taken.

variables.name: FEM_SURG_OTH
variables.description: (for females only) Did you have another type of gynecological surgery?

variables.name: FEM_SURG_OTH_AGE
variables.description: (for females only) How old were you when you first had this other type of gynecological surgery?

variables.name: FOLATE
variables.description: Have you ever taken folic acid or folate pills or tablets at least twice a week for more than a month?

variables.name: FRUIT
variables.description: About two years ago, on average how often did you eat a piece or serving of fruit? A serving of fruit is: 1 medium fresh fruit; 1/2 cup of chopped, cooked or canned fruit; 1/4 cup of dried fruit; 6 ounces of fruit juice

variables.name: FRUIT_INT
variables.description: Interval of frequency fruit

variables.name: GB_REM_AGE
variables.description: How old were you when you had your gallbladder removed?

variables.name: GB_SURG
variables.description: Have you had your gallbladder removed?

variables.name: H1_FAMHX
variables.description: Does the reason for your first hemoccult test include "family history of colorectal cancer"?

variables.name: H1_FU_FOBT
variables.description: Did the reason for your first hemoccult test include "follow-up of a previous hemoccult or fecal occult blood test (FOBT)"?

tables.description: This subject phenotype table includes family IDs, subject IDs, family proband, vital status, age at vital status, number of years between subject's baseline questionnaire completion date and subject's most recent follow-up questionnaire completion date, first, primary or immediate cause of death in International Classification of Diseases (ICD), first, primary or immediate cause of death in text if ICD is not available, first-10th underlying causes of death in ICD, first-10th underlying cause of death in text if no ICD, immediate and underlying cause of death (free text, provided where cause of death has not been coded into COD_IMM_TXT and COD_UND_TXT fields), source of cause of death for all causes primary to 10 underlying, version of ICD for all cause of deaths from causes of death (COD) source, counts of subject's 1st or 2nd degree relatives with at least one diagnosis of malignant colorectal cancer, and counts of subject's 1st or 2nd degree relatives diagnosed with malignant colorectal cancer at ICD-O-3 sites C180-C189,  C199, C209, C218, and C260.

tables.description: This subject phenotype table includes subject IDs, tumor sequential numbers, tumor location, histological type, tumor behavior, age, and source of diagnosis information.

variables.name: AGEDX
variables.description: Age at diagnosis.

variables.name: BEHAV
variables.description: Fifth digit of the ICD-O-3 morphology code which designates the behavior of this tumor. Coding for this field is based on SEER, NAACCR and AcoS guidelines.

variables.name: DXSRC
variables.description: Source of diagnosis information.

variables.name: HISTO
variables.description: First four digits of the ICD-O-3 morphology code which designates the histologic type of this tumor. Coding for this field is based on SEER, NAACCR and AcoS guidelines.

variables.name: SITE
variables.description: Location where this tumor originated in as much detail as is known. An ICD-O-3 code is provided.

variables.name: SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Person identifier uniquely identifying an individual

variables.name: TUMOR_NO
variables.description: Computer generated sequential number, starting with "1", assigned to each tumor for a given individual

variables.name: CONSENT
variables.description: Consent group as determined by DAC

variables.name: SEX
variables.description: Sex of the individual

variables.name: SOURCE_SUBJECT_ID
variables.description: Subject ID used in the Source Repository

variables.description: Subject ID
